steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence introduces Malaysia's single-member plurality voting system. The second sentence explains what that system means in practice: candidates win by receiving a plurality of votes. The transition must signal that the second sentence is restating or defining the concept just named.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In other words,: Signals restatement or clarification, which is exactly the relationship here — the second sentence unpacks what "single-member plurality" means. ✓
  • Choice B — Meanwhile,: Signals simultaneous or parallel events happening at the same time. The second sentence is not describing a separate concurrent event. ✗
  • Choice C — That being said,: Signals a concession or contrast, implying the second sentence contradicts or qualifies the first. No contrast is present. ✗
  • Choice D — Moreover,: Signals addition of a new, distinct point. The second sentence is not adding new information — it is defining the term already introduced. ✗
That is the correct answer.
